# Team Execution

Execute the implementation plan at `$ARGUMENTS` using delegated workers, spec compliance review, quality gates, and live improvement capture.

Core principle: every requirement ID must be implemented exactly as specified and verified against actual code.

## Start Conditions

1. Read the plan file.
2. Fetch and rebase the working branch onto the latest main.
3. Verify the project lint and test commands pass before starting delegated work.

## Delegation

Use Claude teammates from this plugin:

- `implementer-standard`
- `implementer-complex`
- `spec-reviewer`

Spawn example:

```text
Agent(subagent_type="implementer-standard", prompt="...")
```

## Delegation Rules

- `[execution]` `AI-EXEC-001` Use auto execution mode for implementer and reviewer teammates.
- `[execution]` `AI-EXEC-002` Spawn implementers for the first wave only. Reuse idle implementers for later waves via SendMessage with the new task spec.
- `[execution]` `AI-EXEC-003` Before spawning implementers: fetch origin/main, rebase onto origin/main, verify lint and tests pass.
- `[execution]` `AI-EXEC-004` Append to every implementer spawn prompt: Only implement your assigned task. Do not work on tasks assigned to other agents.

## Standing Rules

- `[planning]` `AI-PLAN-001` Include exact definitions, payloads, and downstream-consumed methods for shared types before teammates start.
- `[planning]` `AI-PLAN-002` Put scaffold file creation in the first task that needs it or add a setup task.
- `[testing]` `AI-TEST-001` Plan test infrastructure against every mutable backend the feature uses.
- `[testing]` `AI-TEST-002` Use serial execution controls for integration tests that share mutable backend instances.
- `[testing]` `AI-TEST-003` When adding connection-gating settings, search all state-construction call sites and guard each fixture.
- `[verification]` `AI-VERIFY-001` Let affected implementers defer lint only when failures are outside ownership, then verify lint after the wave completes.
- `[cleanup]` `AI-CLEANUP-001` Add a post-wave cleanup check for temporary dead-code and unused suppressions.
- `[state]` `AI-STATE-001` Prefer one-step atomic mutations for contended shared state.

## Quality Gates

When a worker reports completion:

1. Verify only owned files changed.
2. Verify tests exist for every assigned requirement ID.
3. Run focused tests for the touched behavior.
4. Ask the spec reviewer to inspect actual code, not the worker report.
5. Reject and reassign fixes until the reviewer approves.

## Spec Amendment Flow

If the plan or design doc conflicts with implementation reality:

1. Stop the affected work.
2. Surface the requirement ID, current spec text, discovered issue, and proposed change to the user.
3. Apply the user's decision to the design doc and plan.
4. Broadcast the amendment to affected workers.

Never silently change the spec.

## Publish For Review

Publish the branch and move linked tickets to needs-testing.

Generate the review body first:

Formatter `review_body`:

```jinja
Branch: {{ branch_ref }}

{{ summary }}

## Linked tickets
{{ linked_ticket_ids | ticket_ids_pretty }}
```

Parameters:
- `title`: `plain_text` (string) - Human-facing single value. Not executed as a command.
- `summary`: `markdown_text` (string) - Markdown body content. Write only to a validated body_file path; never interpolate into argv.
- `linked_ticket_ids`: `ticket_id_list` (list) - Non-empty list of adapter-specific ticket IDs.
- `branch_ref`: `git_ref` (string) - Git ref. Must not start with -, contain whitespace, or contain ...

Write the rendered body to a path matching `body_file_path`, then run:

Command `work_publish`:
1. `argv`: `["git", "push", "-u", "origin", "HEAD"]`
2. For each item in `linked_ticket_ids` as `ticket_id`:
   `argv`: `["tkt", "add-note", "<ticket_id>", "<contents of validated body_file>"]` Read `body_file` after path validation and pass the full file contents as that argv element.
3. For each item in `linked_ticket_ids` as `ticket_id`:
   `argv`: `["tkt", "edit", "<ticket_id>", "--status", "needs_testing"]`

Before using values, normalize and validate each argument:

- `title`: `shell_safe_title` (string) - Free text for a CLI title argument. Allowed characters are ASCII letters, digits, spaces, and . , : ; ( ) / _ - @.
- `body_file`: `body_file_path` (string) - Relative path under .agent-skills-tmp/. No absolute paths, no .. segments, filename must start with an alphanumeric and end in .md.
- `linked_ticket_ids`: `ticket_id_list` (list) - Non-empty list of adapter-specific ticket IDs.

Run each argv array directly through the runtime's structured command mechanism. Preserve one argv element per rendered token.

## Retro Capture

Append execution notes to `.agent-skills/retro-notes.md` in the consumer project:

- timestamp and subtask reference
- task duration and reviewer rejection count
- spec amendments
- repeated friction points
- candidate durable process improvements

Do not edit this marketplace repository while executing a consumer project workflow.

## Handoff

Report the review artifact, linked tickets, verification commands run, and `.agent-skills/retro-notes.md` location.
